title: orchestration workflow schema (the steps model)
description: Defines the canonical shape of a workflow.yaml. The single root node is `workflow`; its `steps` constitute the flow. Steps form a flow with three shapes, all expressed through `after` preconditions: FORK = sibling steps sharing one `after`; MERGE = a step with several `after`s; ITERATION/LOOP = the acyclic step body re-executed per item at runtime — the `after` DAG stays acyclic (no graph cycles), the run log tolerates repeated step executions (replay = latest-line-wins, ordering uses first-occurrence), and the loop is bounded by state completion conditions. Each step's obligations are ONE flat `conditions` list. For pre/post checks, `type` is `after` or `state`: `after` binds a predecessor `step_id`; `state` binds a set selector and a predicate (`set_selector` + `set_predicate`). Selector execution is artifact-backed: `set_selector.set_type = artifact`, FROM is declared as `artifact_types` (alias + schema_id), and `set_query` may only reference declared aliases. Invariants remain instruction-based obligations. There is no per-workflow Python. Mediation is an implicit harness function — there is no `facilitate` step kind; participant→participant `after` edges are legal and the harness sequences them. See the harness README Logging section.

identifier.description: A lowercase, hyphen/underscore slug (workflow id, step id).

conditionId.description: A snake_case predicate id, unique within its step; it appears in the run log's conditions[] entries.

artifactTypeRef.description: One FROM binding for artifact selection: alias + schema id.

schema_id.description: Artifact schema identifier selected by this alias.

setSelector.description: Selector contract for `type: state` conditions. FROM is declared via `artifact_types` aliases; `set_query` is a CEL expression returning the selected set and may only reference declared aliases plus runtime constants (unit/product/run scope).

set_type.description: Current selector backend. Only artifact-backed selection is supported.

set_query.description: CEL set query yielding the selected set, using only declared aliases (and runtime constants). Every `<artifact>.<property>` reference is statically checked against the alias's schema — an undeclared property is a hard error.

condition.description: One entry in a step's flat `conditions` list. `kind` = WHEN it holds (precondition / postcondition). For pre/post checks: `type` is either `after` (structural predecessor via `step_id`) or `state` (portfolio-state assertion via `set_selector` + `set_predicate`). Guidance is now at the step level (step.instructions / step.prompts). During migration, expression/value fields are retained for backward compatibility with old conditions.

kind.description: WHEN the condition holds: precondition (before the step acts), postcondition (after), or invariant (legacy, being phased out during migration).

type.description: WHAT the condition is about: `after` (structural predecessor via `step_id`), `state` (portfolio-state via `set_selector` + `set_predicate`), or legacy types for migration (input, output, content, challenge, etc.).

id.description: Stable id, required on EVERY condition — the handle in the run log, findings, and check-step.

step_id.description: For `type: after`: predecessor step id that must be complete.

set_selector.description: For `type: state`: defines the artifact set to evaluate.

set_predicate.description: For `type: state`: a CEL boolean assertion evaluated over the set chosen by `set_selector.set_query`, which it references as `selected`. It may also reference the declared aliases. Every `<artifact>.<property>` reference is statically checked against the alias's schema — an undeclared property is a hard error.

expression.description: Legacy field for migration: ref | cel | instruction. Will be removed when all workflows are migrated.

value.description: Legacy field for migration: condition value (depends on expression type). Will be removed when all workflows are migrated.

step.description: One structurant step (one actor's turn). Its conditions are ONE flat `conditions` list using `type: after|state`: `after` binds `step_id` for structural dependencies, `state` binds `set_selector` + `set_predicate` for portfolio-state assertions. Behavioral guidance (instructions, prompts) is attached at the step level via step.instructions and step.prompts. Exactly one `actor` per step (one actor per turn): a turn taken by several participants is several forked steps, not one. NOTE: a step's own `kind` (author/challenge/delegate/render/gate/commit/verify/direct/…) is the step's nature and is unrelated to a condition's `kind` (precondition/postcondition).

gate.description: Human ★-gate label for a `kind: gate` step (e.g. '★ Epic Gate'). Authoring metadata naming which gate this step represents; the functional trigger is `kind: gate`.

delegates_to.description: For kind: delegate — the sub-workflow this step runs (path under workflows/).

skills.description: The ordered skill ids the dispatched agent loads for THIS step (per-step, not per-workflow). The harness injects them as deterministic context at sessionStart (inject-skills); a sub-orchestration is a step, so its procedure skill rides here.

output.description: The single artifact KIND this step produces or updates (effect-determinism, invariant 15). Resolves to a real, owned, typed artifact via the artifact catalog + ACL.

conditions.description: The step's flat obligations list — each condition is either an `after` dependency, a `state` set-assertion, or an instruction-based invariant.

instructions.description: Step-level guidance injected to the agent at session-open (once preconditions pass). Carries behavioral guidance refs (e.g., authority obligations, discovery lenses). A single string or array of strings, each a contract/repo-relative ref to a `.instructions.md` file.

prompts.description: Step-level prompt guidance injected to the agent at session-open (once preconditions pass). A single string or array of strings, each a contract/repo-relative ref to a `.prompt.md` file.

workflow.description: The single root node. Carries the workflow header (`id`, `facilitator`, optional `kind`/`parent`) plus the `steps` that constitute it. Root workflows are identified by id ∈ {lpm, art, scrum} and carry no parent; sub-workflows declare kind/parent.

steps.description: The structurant steps constituting this workflow (one actor per step; fork/merge/loop via `after`).
